headline: Allbirds rebrands as Smartbird in AI pivot, hires Amazon alum as CEO
summary: STORY: Another AI company is off and running... and, this time, it's coming from former sneaker brand Allbirds.Allbirds on Wednesday officially changed its name to Smartbird, cementing its months-long transformation from shoe maker to AI infrastructure firm and sending its shares up as much as 75%.:: ArchiveThe move comes after the company in April said it was shifting its focus to offer cloud computing capacity and AI services, leading to a more than five-fold jump in its shares. The newly-named Smartbird on Wednesday also announced the appointment of former Amazon executive Nadia Carlsten as its new CEO.:: Amazon Web ServicesCarlsten brings AI and quantum computing experience from Amazon Web Services and Alphabet spinoff SandboxAQ, and has advised the World Economic Forum on computing and AI.In a statement, she said that with a (quote) "differentiated strategy, significant capital, and the opportunity to build an exceptional team, we are uniquely positioned to capitalize on one of the most significant infrastructure opportunities of the next decade."Allbirds shut most of its brick-and-mortar stores and sold its brand and footwear assets in March to American Exchange Group for $39 million.

raw.headline: Allbirds rebrands as Smartbird in AI pivot, hires Amazon alum as CEO
raw.summary: STORY: Another AI company is off and running... and, this time, it's coming from former sneaker brand Allbirds.Allbirds on Wednesday officially changed its name to Smartbird, cementing its months-long transformation from shoe maker to AI infrastructure firm and sending its shares up as much as 75%.:: ArchiveThe move comes after the company in April said it was shifting its focus to offer cloud computing capacity and AI services, leading to a more than five-fold jump in its shares. The newly-named Smartbird on Wednesday also announced the appointment of former Amazon executive Nadia Carlsten as its new CEO.:: Amazon Web ServicesCarlsten brings AI and quantum computing experience from Amazon Web Services and Alphabet spinoff SandboxAQ, and has advised the World Economic Forum on computing and AI.In a statement, she said that with a (quote) "differentiated strategy, significant capital, and the opportunity to build an exceptional team, we are uniquely positioned to capitalize on one of the most significant infrastructure opportunities of the next decade."Allbirds shut most of its brick-and-mortar stores and sold its brand and footwear assets in March to American Exchange Group for $39 million.

headline: AI lab Odyssey valued at $1.45 billion in latest funding round
summary: AI lab Odyssey on Wednesday said it has raised $310 million in a Series B round valuing it at $1.45 ‌billion, and struck a deal with Amazon Web Services that gives ‌it access to specialized chips designed for high‑performance machine-learning workloads.  The round, led by Natural Capital, ​saw participation from Amazon, AMD Ventures, Google Ventures, EQT and In-Q-Tel, alongside prominent individual investors including Google’s chief scientist Jeff Dean and Garry Tan, president and CEO of Y Combinator.  Odyssey is building models that can ⁠simulate the real world and act without human input.

headline: Oracle Stock's High Stakes Gamble
summary: If you hold Oracle (ORCL) stock, you’ve felt the turbulence. Despite a recent bounce, the shares are down over the last 12 months and sit well below their peak, a sign the market is wrestling with a profound shift in the company’s story. The options market is pricing in elevated uncertainty, with implied volatility in the 67th percentile of its annual range. This isn't about a legacy business in decline; it's about the significant risks of a company going all-in on a new one.

This isn't about a legacy business in decline; it's about the significant risks of a company going all-in on a new one.

headline: Intel (INTC) Moves 18A P Into Risk Production As AI Partnerships Expand
summary: Intel (NasdaqGS:INTC) has moved its next-generation 18A-P manufacturing process into risk production. The 18A-P process is aimed at advanced AI and data center chips and is designed to be backward compatible for customers. Intel is working with Foxconn on AI infrastructure and with Kontron AG on early edge AI deployments. For investors watching Intel, the shift of 18A-P into risk production is a data point in the company’s attempt to build a foundry business alongside its existing processor...

headline: Uber Robo-Taxis Move Step Closer as Self-Driving War Revs Up
summary: On Wednesday, ride-hailing giant  Uber  EV maker  Lucid  and self-driving technology start-up Nuro announced plans to launch robo-taxis in Houston in 2027.  Uber operates the network, Lucid builds the cars, and Nuro provides the autonomous driving technology.  The move is yet another example that the long-promised era of driverless cars has arrived.

headline: KLIPY raises $3.8M from investors and joins Google AI Futures Fund to build the expression layer for GIFs, memes, stickers, clips, and AI media
summary: KLIPY today announced a $3.8 million fundraising round, and that it has joined Google's AI Futures Fund, to scale its expression API layer for memes, stickers, clips, and AI for real-time expression. KLIPY helps developers integrate expressive media and AI APIs into social and creative platforms like BeReal, Baidu, Canva, Figma, and Microsoft SwiftKey, while giving creators, brands, and rights holders tools to distribute, manage, and control content.

KLIPY helps developers integrate expressive media and AI APIs into social and creative platforms like BeReal, Baidu, Canva, Figma, and Microsoft SwiftKey, while giving creators, brands, and rights holders tools to distribute, manage, and control content.

headline: UK orders Google to improve search transparency to aid competition
summary: STORY: Britain’s competition watchdog has ordered Google to give greater transparency on how its search rankings work.It’s part of new rules aimed at securing a "fairer deal" for businesses.And addresses concerns over the U.S. tech giant's dominance in the sector.The regulator said Google must also rank organic search results using objective criteria and introduce clearer complaint processes. Google said it will work with the watchdog, adding that its ranking systems are fair and transparent.These new measures follow UK watchdog requirements announced earlier this month, which enable publishers to stop their content being used to power Google's AI features.Google in March said it was developing new search controls to address British competition concerns.The regulator said Google has six months to implement the fair ranking requirement - and warns of further measures if necessary. This adds to the increasing regulatory scrutiny the tech giant is facing around the world, including in the U.S. and EU.

raw.headline: UK orders Google to improve search transparency to aid competition
raw.summary: STORY: Britain’s competition watchdog has ordered Google to give greater transparency on how its search rankings work.It’s part of new rules aimed at securing a "fairer deal" for businesses.And addresses concerns over the U.S. tech giant's dominance in the sector.The regulator said Google must also rank organic search results using objective criteria and introduce clearer complaint processes. Google said it will work with the watchdog, adding that its ranking systems are fair and transparent.These new measures follow UK watchdog requirements announced earlier this month, which enable publishers to stop their content being used to power Google's AI features.Google in March said it was developing new search controls to address British competition concerns.The regulator said Google has six months to implement the fair ranking requirement - and warns of further measures if necessary. This adds to the increasing regulatory scrutiny the tech giant is facing around the world, including in the U.S. and EU.

headline: AI startup Genspark valued at $2.6 billion in latest funding round
summary: Technology company Genspark.ai said on Wednesday it had raised $100 million in its extended funding round ‌that valued it at $2.6 billion, underscoring strong investor appetite ‌for AI companies developing workplace productivity tools. The round was backed by existing investors ​including Sozo Ventures, Korea Mirae Asset and UpHonest Capital. The latest investment brings the Palo Alto, California-based company's total Series B financing to $485 million.

headline: Big Tech-backed coalition for carbon removal increases funding by $915 million, adds Anthropic
summary: A coalition backed by Big Tech companies for ​carbon removal technology, said on Wednesday it would inject an additional $915 million into the sector, and added Anthropic as a new participant. Frontier, launched in ‌2022 by companies ⁠including Stripe and Google, aims to help scale carbon removal technologies by committing ⁠to buy credits in advance, thereby derisking the projects and helping them grow more quickly. The additional ​funding brings ​its total funding ​pledges to $1.8 billion.

summary: Adoption of artificial intelligence has reached a "tipping point" in Britain, as companies move from experimentation ‌to large-scale deployment and start seeing returns, a Google Cloud executive ‌said on Wednesday.  Businesses and government bodies that were testing AI tools a year ago are now ​using them to run more complex processes and improve productivity, Maureen Costello, Google Cloud's vice president for the United Kingdom, Ireland and Sub-Saharan Africa, told Reuters in an interview.

headline: UK regulator sets out new rules on Google Search to boost competition
summary: Britain's competition watchdog ordered Google to provide greater transparency on ‌how its search rankings work, part of ‌new requirements aimed at securing "a fairer deal" for businesses and ​improving the U.S. tech giant's services in the country.  The first requires Google to improve transparency and fairness in how search results are ranked, while ‌the second requires ⁠Google to allow users to move their search data to authorised third parties, ⁠the Competition and Markets Authority said in a statement on Wednesday.  The regulator has flagged concerns about ​Google's dominance ​in search, designating ​the company with "strategic market ‌status", which allows it to set targeted rules to increase transparency.

raw.headline: UK regulator sets out new rules on Google Search to boost competition
raw.summary: Britain's competition watchdog ordered Google to provide greater transparency on ‌how its search rankings work, part of ‌new requirements aimed at securing "a fairer deal" for businesses and ​improving the U.S. tech giant's services in the country.  The first requires Google to improve transparency and fairness in how search results are ranked, while ‌the second requires ⁠Google to allow users to move their search data to authorised third parties, ⁠the Competition and Markets Authority said in a statement on Wednesday.  The regulator has flagged concerns about ​Google's dominance ​in search, designating ​the company with "strategic market ‌status", which allows it to set targeted rules to increase transparency.

headline: Sanjay Poonen, Cohesity CEO: A Fortt Knox Update
summary: Cohesity CEO Sanjay Poonen told Jon Fortt the company is launching Maestro, a headless architecture that lets Cohesity's cyber-resilience capabilities be invoked by any agent orchestrator: Claude, ChatGPT, or enterprise platforms. Poonen called it the next step after Cohesity Copilot and the recovery agent, riffing on Ben Franklin to argue an ounce of resilience equals a pound of detection as attackers use agentic AI to compress attack timelines.

headline: Tim Draper Says The Next AI Giant May Not Be OpenAI Or Anthropic: 'We're Looking Around The Periphery'
summary: Tim Draper says investors seeking the next AI winner should look beyond OpenAI, Anthropic and xAI to AI applications.

headline: Dow Jones Futures Rise Before Warsh's Fed Debut; SpaceX Keeps Rising, Four Stocks In Buy Areas
summary: The Dow Jones hit a new high and SpaceX kept rising. But chips and most AI stocks fell. Fed chief Kevin Warsh's comments will be in focus Wednesday.

headline: Elon Musk's SpaceX Dominates Retail Trading, But Analyst Sees Echoes Of The Meme Stock Era
summary: Retail investors are driving up demand for SpaceX stock, but one analyst warns it could turn into a meme stock.
